scenario: During a Camunda 8.8 rollout at Granite Insurance covering 4800 instances, an operator has just clicked Apply inside the summary modal on one instance. The operations team wants to understand exactly what feedback Operate gives when the modification operation is created successfully and how quickly the changes show up.
question: What does Operate show when the modification operation is created successfully from the summary modal?

options.text: Operate shows a success notification and the staged changes are then reflected on the instance in a short time once the modification operation has been created successfully from the summary modal
Operate redirects the operator out of the instance view to a dedicated Operations history page, where a completion bar moves from 0 percent to 100 percent before the changes appear on the instance
Operate requires the operator to manually run a Sync Now action on the cluster console before any of the staged changes become visible on the affected single process instance in the view
Operate refuses to apply the changes until every other operator currently viewing the same instance has explicitly approved the pending modification set from their own session of Operate

a.text: Correct. The docs say that on a successful apply from the summary modal the operator sees a success notification and changes are reflected on the instance in a short time, without further manual steps from the operator.

b.text: Incorrect. Operate stays in the instance view and shows a success notification on a successful apply; there is no documented redirect to an Operations history page with a percentage completion bar.

c.text: Incorrect. Changes are reflected in a short time after a successful apply; there is no documented Sync Now action on the cluster console required to surface the staged changes on the instance.

d.text: Incorrect. The documented apply flow does not require explicit approval from other operators viewing the instance before the modification operation can be created and reflected on the instance.

explanation: After Apply, if the modification operation is created successfully, Operate displays a success notification and the staged changes are reflected on the instance in a short time. No additional manual sync or cross-operator approval is required.
